title: Pervasive Demographic Synchrony Across the Holocene: Evidence from 166,000 Radiocarbon Dates Spanning Both Hemispheres
abstract: Using 166,000+ radiocarbon dates from p3k14c across ten regions spanning both hemispheres, we document pervasive positive demographic synchrony (mean detrended r = 0.458 for 7 Northern Hemisphere pairs; NH x SH mean r = 0.439 using different calibration curves). This synchrony survives seven robustness tests: Bartlett correction, block bootstrap, loess detrending, taphonomic correction (r = 0.419), calibration artifact null (p < 0.001), equal-sample subsampling (r = 0.307), and a cross-hemisphere test using SHCal20 (9/9 pairs p < 0.001) that definitively rules out IntCal20 calibration artifacts. Spectral decomposition reveals synchrony is concentrated at centennial scales (100-500yr, r = 0.420, all pairs positive) while the 500-1500yr band where climate forcing should operate shows near-zero synchrony (r = 0.057), constituting a frequency-domain falsification of simple climate models. Peak synchrony at the Late Bronze Age (~2,600-2,950 cal BP) provides quantitative evidence of pan-Eurasian demographic coupling.
novelty_statement: First systematic cross-continental and cross-hemisphere analysis of detrended SPD synchrony. Three key innovations: (1) cross-hemisphere SHCal20 test definitively ruling out calibration artifacts, (2) spectral decomposition revealing synchrony lives at the wrong frequency for climate forcing, (3) seven independent robustness tests including taphonomic correction and equal-sample subsampling.
